dvs Error Reference

every error dvs can raise, shown side-by-side for R and the CLI

Setup

library(dvs)
library(here)
here() starts at /Users/elea/Documents/a2ai_github/dvs2-demo-errors

Create a temporary storage directory and project root, then expose them to the shell so the bash chunks below can use $DVS_PROJECT and $DVS_STORAGE.

storage     <- tempfile(fileext = "_storage", tmpdir = here::here())
new_project <- tempfile(fileext = "_project", tmpdir = here::here())

dir.create(storage)
dir.create(new_project)

# Make it a git repo so dvs can find the project root
system2("git", c("init", new_project), stdout = FALSE, stderr = FALSE)

Sys.setenv(DVS_PROJECT = new_project, DVS_STORAGE = storage)

init errors

Storage path is inside the repository

Passing a storage path that resolves to a subdirectory of the project root is rejected — dvs refuses to version-control its own blob store.

setwd(new_project)
dvs_init(storage_path = file.path(new_project, "inside_storage"),
         root_dir     = new_project)
Error in `dvs_init_impl()`:
! The given storage path is within the repository.
cd "$DVS_PROJECT"
dvs init "$DVS_PROJECT/inside_storage"
The given storage path is within the repository.

Project already initialised — dvs.toml exists

Once dvs.toml is present a second init call fails immediately.

# First init succeeds
setwd(new_project)
dvs_init(storage_path = storage, root_dir = new_project)
DVS Initialized
# Second init fails
dvs_init(storage_path = storage, root_dir = new_project)
Error in `dvs_init_impl()`:
! dvs is already initialized (dvs.toml exists)
# dvs.toml already created by the R chunk above
cd "$DVS_PROJECT"
dvs init "$DVS_STORAGE"
dvs is already initialized (dvs.toml exists)

Errors not demonstrated here

  • Backend storage already exists (dvs is already initialized (backend storage exists)): triggered by removing dvs.toml and .dvs/ while leaving the storage directory intact, then re-running init. Requires filesystem surgery; straightforward to contrive but not illuminating.

add errors

Not inside a DVS project

Calling dvs_add / dvs add from a directory that has no dvs.toml anywhere in its ancestor chain fails before touching the filesystem.

# Use a fresh temp dir with no dvs.toml
outside <- tempfile(tmpdir = here::here())
dir.create(outside)
setwd(outside)

dvs_add(paths = "anything.csv")
Error in `dvs_add_impl()`:
! Not in a DVS repository
OUTSIDE=$(mktemp -d)
cd "$OUTSIDE"
dvs add anything.csv
rm -rf "$OUTSIDE"
Not in a DVS repository

No files to add — empty glob

A glob pattern that matches no files returns an empty set; dvs raises an error rather than silently doing nothing.

setwd(new_project)
dvs_add(glob = "data/*.csv")
Error in `dvs_add_impl()`:
! No files to add
cd "$DVS_PROJECT"
dvs add --glob "data/*.csv"
No files to add

File does not exist

Requesting a specific path that is not present on disk is a per-file error; the overall command exits non-zero.

setwd(new_project)
dvs_add(paths = "nonexistent_file.csv")
Error in `dvs_add_impl()`:
! Path not found: nonexistent_file.csv
cd "$DVS_PROJECT"
dvs add nonexistent_file.csv
Path not found: nonexistent_file.csv

Path resolves to a directory, not a file

Passing a plain directory name (without a glob) is rejected.

setwd(new_project)
dir.create(file.path(new_project, "mydir"), showWarnings = FALSE)
dvs_add(paths = "mydir")
Error in `dvs_add_impl()`:
! No files to add
cd "$DVS_PROJECT"
mkdir -p mydir
dvs add mydir
No files to add

Path is outside the project root

An explicit path that canonicalises to a location above (or beside) the project root is rejected with a per-file error.

setwd(new_project)
outside_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".csv")
writeLines("a,b,c", outside_file)
# Use a relative path that escapes the project root
rel_outside <- file.path("..", basename(outside_file))
dvs_add(paths = rel_outside)
Error in `dvs_add_impl()`:
! Path not found: ../file11eab273319bd.csv
cd "$DVS_PROJECT"
OUTSIDE_FILE=$(mktemp --suffix=.csv)
echo "a,b,c" > "$OUTSIDE_FILE"
dvs add "$OUTSIDE_FILE"
mktemp: unrecognized option `--suffix=.csv'
usage: mktemp [-d] [-p tmpdir] [-q] [-t prefix] [-u] template ...
       mktemp [-d] [-p tmpdir] [-q] [-u] -t prefix 
bash: line 3: : No such file or directory
error: a value is required for '[PATHS]...' but none was supplied

For more information, try '--help'.

Errors not demonstrated here

  • Path not found during glob resolution (Path not found: <path>): fired by resolve_paths_for_add when an explicit path argument does not exist on disk at all (distinct from a valid-but-untracked path). The “file does not exist” demo above exercises the post-resolution file not found variant; the pre-resolution path is harder to distinguish at the R / shell surface level.
  • Failed to resolve path (symlink / IO error): requires a broken symlink or unavailable mount; environment-specific.

status errors

Not inside a DVS project

dvs_status / dvs status require a dvs.toml in the ancestor tree.

outside2 <- tempfile(tmpdir = here::here())
dir.create(outside2)
setwd(outside2)
dvs_status()
Error in `dvs_status_impl()`:
! Not in a DVS repository
OUTSIDE2=$(mktemp -d)
cd "$OUTSIDE2"
dvs status
rm -rf "$OUTSIDE2"
Not in a DVS repository

Errors not demonstrated here

  • Unparseable meta file (error: failed to determine relative path / serde JSON error): requires manually corrupting a .dvs metadata file. The error surfaces as a per-file StatusDetail::Error row rather than a top-level abort.

get errors

Not inside a DVS project

outside3 <- tempfile(tmpdir = here::here())
dir.create(outside3)
setwd(outside3)
dvs_get(paths = "something.csv")
Error in `dvs_get_impl()`:
! Not in a DVS repository
OUTSIDE3=$(mktemp -d)
cd "$OUTSIDE3"
dvs get something.csv
rm -rf "$OUTSIDE3"
Not in a DVS repository

No files to get — empty glob

When the glob resolves to zero tracked files, dvs get aborts.

setwd(new_project)
# No files have been added yet under data/; glob matches nothing in metadata
dvs_get(glob = "data/nope_*.csv")
Error in `dvs_get_impl()`:
! No files to get
cd "$DVS_PROJECT"
dvs get --glob "data/nope_*.csv"
No files to get

File is not tracked by DVS

Requesting a file that exists on disk but has never been dvs add-ed.

setwd(new_project)
writeLines("col1,col2", file.path(new_project, "untracked.csv"))
dvs_get(paths = "untracked.csv")
Error in `dvs_get_impl()`:
! No files to get
cd "$DVS_PROJECT"
echo "col1,col2" > untracked.csv
dvs get untracked.csv
No files to get

File is not found at all

Requesting a path that neither exists on disk nor has metadata.

setwd(new_project)
dvs_get(paths = "ghost.csv")
Error in `dvs_get_impl()`:
! No files to get
cd "$DVS_PROJECT"
dvs get ghost.csv
No files to get

Storage blob missing for tracked file

The metadata .dvs file exists but the corresponding blob has been deleted from storage — simulating a corrupted or partially-synced store.

setwd(new_project)

# Add a real file so we have metadata
writeLines("x,y,z", file.path(new_project, "tracked.csv"))
dvs_add(paths = "tracked.csv")
# A tibble: 1 × 5
  path        outcome hash                                     size  stored_size
  <chr>       <chr>   <chr>                                    <dvs> <dvs_byts> 
1 tracked.csv copied  1357bc0161e229426dd1fba64a6adfd193f6676… 6 B   15 B       
# Remove the local copy so get actually attempts a retrieve
file.remove(file.path(new_project, "tracked.csv"))
[1] TRUE
# Delete every blob from storage so the retrieve has nothing to copy
blob_dirs <- list.dirs(storage, recursive = TRUE, full.names = TRUE)
blob_files <- list.files(storage, recursive = TRUE, full.names = TRUE)
blob_files <- blob_files[!grepl("audit", blob_files)]
invisible(file.remove(blob_files))

# Now get should fail: metadata exists but blob is gone
dvs_get(paths = "tracked.csv")
# A tibble: 1 × 2
  path        error                                                             
  <chr>       <chr>                                                             
1 tracked.csv Storage file missing for hash: Hashes(blake3=1357bc0161e229426dd1…
cd "$DVS_PROJECT"
# tracked.csv was deleted and its blob removed by the R chunk above
dvs get tracked.csv
Error: tracked.csv - Storage file missing for hash: Hashes(blake3=1357bc0161e229426dd1fba64a6adfd193f667632c54a5614ebde7370b700a5a)
Some files failed to get

Errors not demonstrated here

  • Retrieved file does not match expected hash (Retrieved file does not match expected hash): requires writing a blob with corrupted content under the correct hash path, so decompression succeeds but the post-retrieve hash check fails. Needs direct filesystem manipulation of the content-addressed store.

Cleanup

setwd(here::here())
unlink(new_project, recursive = TRUE)
unlink(storage,     recursive = TRUE)
